items.prompt: interpret common percents as rates per hundred and represent them in fraction, decimal, or hundred-model form
items.expected_evidence: use hundred grids, benchmark percents, and equivalent forms to reason about common percents
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner represents percent as an amount out of one hundred rather than as a detached symbol.

items.prompt: explain how a percent, an equivalent fraction, and a decimal can describe the same bounded quantity
items.expected_evidence: explain that 25% means 25 out of 100 and connect it to 1/4 and 0.25 in bounded cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains percent through rate-per-hundred and equivalent-form structure.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to symbol_without_quantity.
items.expected_evidence: solve bounded percent-of-whole and what-percent questions without collapsing percent into unrelated whole-number operations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies why the peer lost the meaning of percent as rate per hundred.

items.prompt: succeeds across percent-of-whole, part-percent, benchmark discount, and simple data-description contexts
items.expected_evidence: compare percent situations across tables, visuals, and contextual descriptions
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves percent reasoning across representation and context changes.
